# MANIFESTO_WRITER_BRIEF.md

**Anchored to:** CORE_v4_Manifesto_Skeleton_v0_27.md (8,627 lines, 2026-05-17)
**Generated by:** Hostile Auditor (Claude) per Sovereign Q-T3 + Q-T7 + Q-T9 ratification; line-shift patched 2026-05-17 for v0.27 §6.2.3.5 + §6.5.10 + §6.6.4 expansion (+617 lines integrating R3.9, R3.10, R3.11)
**Status:** Authoritative drafting brief for Forger Pool authorship of the v4 paper.
**Companion to:** SKELETON_SPLIT_STRATEGY.md (the 19-file partition specification)
**Supersedes:** All prior MANIFESTO_WRITER_BRIEF.md versions anchored to v0.17 / v0.22 / v0.23 / v0.25 / v0.26.

---

## Purpose

SKELETON_SPLIT_STRATEGY.md specifies WHICH sections go to which drafter and HOW the partition cuts. This brief specifies HOW each section should be drafted — tone, audience, citation discipline, audit-trail-preservation requirements, and Hostile Auditor expectations the drafted prose must satisfy on review.

The recursive thesis governs all drafting: **CORE writes the paper, the paper specifies what CORE builds, CORE builds itself.** Every drafted section must serve as input to CORE's own future architectural development, not just as external publication.

---

## Universal Drafting Principles (Apply To All 19 Files)

### Tone
- **Honest first, polished second.** Every claim cites primary source or flags itself as synthesis. Honest limits are stated inline, not relegated to footnotes.
- **Source-faithful, not source-bound.** Where CORE extends a source (e.g., §5.2.2 PROGRS centering adapted to inference-time MCTS PUCT, §6.5.7 timeline/toolcalls schema split), the extension is acknowledged as CORE original synthesis with the source attribution kept honest about what the source actually specifies.
- **Operational, not speculative.** Future-work commitments belong in §14.5 + §14.6; drafted sections describe what v4 ships, not what some future version might.
- **Skeleton density preserved.** The skeleton is dense by design (concrete API names, specific numerical values, primary-source citations). Prose drafting expands the skeleton's bullets into paragraphs without diluting concreteness.

### Audience
- **Primary audience:** CORE itself, future Forger Pool instances reading the paper to inform architectural development. Every cross-reference must resolve unambiguously; every claim must be re-verifiable against primary source; every honest limit must be preserved with original framing intact.
- **Secondary audience:** AI safety / governance research community capable of consuming dense technical prose. Reference is "ASPLOS / NeurIPS systems track" register, not "blog post" register.
- **Explicitly NOT the audience:** general technical readers requiring tutorial introduction to LLMs / MCTS / cgroups / LoRA / etc. The paper assumes baseline familiarity and works from there.

### Citation discipline
- Every cited paper must use the verified arXiv ID from Open Items Register Category A (v0.25 file lines ~6528-6543)
- Citation format: `(Lastname et al. YEAR, arXiv:XXXX.YYYYY)` for primary source; `(Lastname YEAR)` only for already-cited works on second reference
- **Forbidden:** invented arXiv IDs, year drift, author-name drift, secondary-source citation without primary verification
- **Required for any borderline claim:** primary-source quotation OR explicit "[SYNTHESIS]" flag with rationale
- **Category D bibliography pulls** (Snowflake Arctic-Text2SQL-R1-14B paper / Llama 3.1 8B model card / DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B model card / 30+ Branch corpus papers): drafted prose may USE these citations; the REFERENCES section build is a separate mechanical pass that can run in parallel

### Audit-trail-preservation requirements
- **Three classes of audit-trail content the drafter MUST consume but MUST NOT recreate from scratch:**

  1. **Fabrications-stripped** (e.g., vAttention RoPE-commutativity claim from Gemini v0.20 blueprint Item 3, vAttention tmpfs/SafeTensors framing from same source, Six Preventions repackaging from Gemini v0.20 blueprint Items 2 and 4) — preserved in §6.3.5.5 + §8.5 cross-references for future Forger Pool reference. Drafter MUST NOT reintroduce these claims under any framing. **CRITICAL CLARIFICATION FROM v0.25:** the v0.20.2 fabrication strip on tmpfs+SafeTensors was scoped to GEMINI's UNATTRIBUTED REPACKAGING of R1.2 content — the underlying tmpfs+SafeTensors+BLAKE3 architecture is source-faithful to R1.2 primary source and remains the v4 commitment (per §6.5.2 + §6.5.2.6). Drafter must preserve this clarification: the strip was about ATTRIBUTION, not about ARCHITECTURE.

  2. **Verified corrections** (e.g., LoCM operator weights ¬=1.5 / →=2.0 / ↔=2.0 / ⊕=3.0 from arXiv:2601.02902 Round 5 closure; Tam et al. JSON degradation 27-40 percentage points NOT 10-15%; aLoRA 10-30x NOT 58x; PROGRS as training-time GRPO only NOT inference-time MCTS centering; ReST-MCTS does NOT specify centering) — drafter MUST use the verified values verbatim.

  3. **Synthesis-honest framings** (e.g., CORE's §5.2.2 inference-time MCTS PUCT centering is original synthesis adapted from PROGRS training-time concept; §6.5.7 timeline/toolcalls schema split is CORE architectural extension of R3.4 single-table source; §6.6.3 UAS "2-4ms latency" framed as synthesis estimate awaiting Section 9 measurement) — drafter MUST preserve the honest framing, not collapse it into "as established in [Source]" attribution.

### Length discipline
- Section-level word-count targets are specified in skeleton `### N. SECTION_TITLE (~XXX words)` headers
- Sub-section drafting expands proportionally: a subsection that occupies 15% of section line budget should consume ~15% of section word budget
- **Historical drift warning:** Hostile Auditor prior drafts ran 60-90% over target (e.g., SECTION_08B drafted 2,963 words against 1,400-word target). Drafter must monitor word count actively; tightening passes preferred over deletion passes when target is exceeded
- **MANIFEST WORD_COUNT_ACTUAL** field captures actual drafted count for Hostile Auditor mechanical review

### v0.25 Wilderness Corpus Closed framing
The v0.25 skeleton ships with WILDERNESS CORPUS CLOSED ratified (Q-T5 2026-05-12). 11 primary-source Wilderness papers integrated. Drafter must NOT propose additional Wilderness ingestion within v4 scope; deferred deepening flows through §14.5 Constitutional Re-Audit. If a drafter encounters an apparent architectural gap during drafting, the appropriate response is to flag it for Hostile Auditor review — NOT to compensate by inventing additional source attribution.

## Per-Section Drafting Briefs

### File 01: SECTION_01_INTRODUCTION.md

**Skeleton lines:** 24-60 (~800 words target)
**Status:** Section structure ratified through v0.21 promotion-pass; drafting clean
**Drafter:** Forger Pool

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- Open with the recursive thesis statement, NOT with hardware constraints. The paper is fundamentally about CORE writing CORE; reference platform hardware is a constraint that shapes the architecture, not the thesis itself.
- §1.1 market context can cite Sycamore Labs $65M / Coatue+Lightspeed verified per Brief 5.4. Use SiliconANGLE March 30 2026 OR TechCrunch via Tech in Asia March 30 2026 as primary source URL.
- Three Subsystems framing (Sovereign / Hostile Auditor / Strategic Advisor) introduced HERE as authoring framework, then formalized in §4.1 Six Preventions doctrine. The introduction names them; §4.1 specifies them.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- Recursive thesis quoted verbatim somewhere in the introduction: "CORE writes the paper, the paper specifies what CORE builds, CORE builds itself."
- Introduction must NOT preview Section 14.6 Self-Dispatching Loop Case Study in detail — only the recursive thesis as the framing motivation.

---

### File 02: SECTION_02_RELATED_WORK.md

**Skeleton lines:** 61-72 (~600 words target)
**Status:** Section structure ratified; positioning depends on prior sections
**Drafter:** Forger Pool — DRAFT LAST (positions all prior contributions)

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- Related work positions CORE's contribution against existing literature, not enumeration of papers. Each cited work should be paired with an explicit "CORE differs by..." sentence.
- Constitutional AI (Bai et al. 2022) and Multi-agent debate (Irving et al. 2018) are anchor points; the paper's contribution is positioning constitutional governance at the operational layer rather than the policy layer.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- Every cited paper must use verified arXiv ID; no citation drift permitted.
- §2.6 explicitly frames the gap CORE fills: combining constitutional governance with sovereign-edge implementation. Drafter must NOT broaden the gap statement (e.g., "CORE fills the AI safety gap" — too broad).

---

### File 03: SECTION_03_BIDIRECTIONAL_VERIFICATION.md

**Skeleton lines:** 73-87 (~800 words target)
**Status:** Section structure ratified; depends on §4.1 Prevention 6 specification
**Drafter:** Forger Pool

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- Wright Holmström-Milgrom asymmetry framing is the academic anchor. Establish that the same epistemic problem appears in multi-agent LLM verification.
- The Three Roles (Sovereign / Hostile Auditor / Strategic Advisor) are formalized HERE. §4.1 Prevention 6 codifies the doctrine; this section explains why the doctrine exists.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- Wright name-collision caveat preserved: arXiv:2603.28063 author distinct from controversial Satoshi claimant; epistemic logic foundations cite-independent.
- Doctrine framing must accommodate recursive application: the Hostile Auditor self-audit (Honest Audit 2026-05-07) is the canonical demonstration that the doctrine applies to ALL three Roles including the Hostile Auditor itself.
- Three failure modes (over-attribution / repackaging / fabrication) introduced here; §8.5 catalogs documented instances.

### File 04: SECTION_04_CONSTITUTIONAL_GOVERNANCE.md

**Skeleton lines:** 88-219 (~1500 words target — up from 1200 in v0.23 due to expanded specifications)
**Status:** Section structure FROZEN per §14.5 Constitutional Re-Audit scope
**Drafter:** Forger Pool

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- This is the load-bearing constitutional section. Every other section cites the Six Preventions; every architectural decision derives from them.
- Tone is more formal than other sections — closer to legal/constitutional drafting register than technical-systems register.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- The Six Preventions are NON-NEGOTIABLE. Drafter must NOT propose modifications, additions, or "v0.25 enhancements" to the Preventions. Modifications belong exclusively to §14.5 Constitutional Re-Audit scope.
- Each Prevention specification cites its production failure mode origin: Prevention 1 ↔ over-attribution; Prevention 2 ↔ vendor monoculture risk; Prevention 3 ↔ scope drift; Prevention 4 ↔ infinite regeneration loops; Prevention 5 ↔ autonomous-mutation risk; Prevention 6 ↔ Strategic Advisor hallucination. Drafted prose preserves these origin pairs.
- Citations: arXiv:2506.17331 (Wright Subsystem M); NASA-STD-8739.8; arXiv:2512.02731 (Chojecki GVU); SICA + Kitchen Loop literature.

### File 08: SECTION_06A_INTELLIGENCE_ROUTER.md

**Skeleton lines:** 901-1820 (§6.1 + §6.2 + §6.2.3 aLoRA + §6.2.3.5 R3.9 Cross-LoRA Math NEW v0.27 + §6.2.5 Oracle + §6.2.6 R1 Primary-Source Foundations + §6.3 + §6.3.1-3 + §6.3.2.2.1 R2.3 operational depth; ~3300 words target — expanded for R3.9 tensor-level math integration)
**Status:** Largest single-section drafting target; may split internally if needed
**Drafter:** Forger Pool

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- This section is the architectural backbone — Intelligence Router three phases, STASIS_BATCHER mechanics, Centralized Oracle topology, Cloud Oracle deferred-future-work path. Tone is technical-systems-paper register.
- §6.2.6 R1 Primary-Source Foundations supplies the analytical justification for every architectural decision in §6.2 — drafter weaves the math through the prose rather than treating §6.2.6 as a separate "appendix" feel.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- Cloud Oracle path framed as DEFERRED FUTURE WORK. §6.3.2.2.1 R2.3 operational depth (Prεεmpt + AIOpsShield + DOGe + Helper String Ψ + FPE rationale + LM head fine-tune mask) is the implementation specification for when Sovereign eventually ratifies the path — NOT a v4 architectural commitment.
- STASIS_BATCHER's four-stage gate sequence preserved exactly: Thompson MAB → Betweenness-Centrality → Content-Addressable Deduplication → Model Target Batching.
- aLoRA (arXiv:2504.12397) load-bearing nature acknowledged with the verified 10-30x inference speedup figure (NOT 58x).
- §6.2.6.5 R1 Top-Three Ranking treated as analytical cross-reference for Pool composition — Gemma 4 E4B noted as documented §14.5 Constitutional Re-Audit upgrade candidate, NOT v4 architectural revision.
- §6.2.6.4 GQA/SwiGLU compensation math (SwiGLU(x) = (Swish_β(xW_1+b_1)) ⊗ (xW_2+b_2); 4xy = (x+y)² − (x−y)² polynomial expressivity) preserved as architectural justification, not bare quotation — integrate into prose.
- **§6.2.3.5 R3.9 Cross-LoRA KV Cache Contamination (NEW v0.27):** drafter MUST reproduce the four-term dot-product expansion verbatim:
  - Q_Sec · K_Syn^T = (xW₀,Q + xB_Sec,Q·A_Sec,Q) · (xW₀,K + xB_Syn,K·A_Syn,K)^T
  - Yields Term 1 (Base-Base, valid) + Terms 2/3/4 (cross-terms, parasitic)
  - aLoRA mathematical resolution: cross-terms reduce from four to two; cache content remains adapter-agnostic; O(N) reconstruction guarantee
  - §6.2.3.5.3 disaggregated cache (ForkKV / LRAgent / ResidualAttention / Flash-LoRA-Attention) MUST be framed as v5 Candidate A enabler per §14.5.1, NOT v4 architectural commitment
  - §6.2.3.5.4 base-model forced recalculation rejection rationale preserved (2 GB activation memory + 3-7s prefill latency + stacking on §6.5.9 5-7s swap window = catastrophic)
  - §6.2.3.5.5 llama.cpp implementation barriers preserved as Section 9 cross-adapter swap correctness benchmark target — drafter MUST NOT claim CORE has tested this; the test is a Phase 1 deployment benchmark

### File 10: SECTION_06C_AGENTFS_BLAKE3.md

**Skeleton lines:** 2248-3372 (§6.5 AgentFS with §6.5.1-5.9 + §6.5.10 R3.10 Concurrency NEW v0.27 — substantial expansion from v0.23 due to §6.5.2.6 + §6.5.7.1 + §6.5.7.2 + §6.5.9 + §6.5.10; ~2700 words target — expanded for R3.10 MPSC daemon + WAL tuning + crash-durability integration)
**Status:** Largest §6 subsection set after §6.8
**Drafter:** Forger Pool

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- §6.5 is the State Persistence subsystem from §5.0 — drafted prose should make this subsystem-mapping explicit early.
- §6.5.2 BLAKE3 verification framed as ARCHITECTURALLY LOAD-BEARING (not optional) because consumer Pascal silicon has NO ECC memory (§6.5.2.6.1) — drafter weaves this analytical thread through.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- §6.5.9 Application-Layer Hibernation six-stage sequence preserved exactly: Pause Batcher → Flush CUDA → Export KV via LMCache → Sleep API → NVML Verification → Spin Up Secondary.
- Ollama keep_alive REJECTED for production §5.1 — drafter preserves the zombie-runner failure-mode framing per R1.3 source.
- §6.5.7.2 Privacy-Preserving Schema Extraction: SQLAlchemy inspect() methodology extracts STRUCTURAL BLUEPRINT ONLY. Drafter MUST NOT collapse this into "schema-aware T2SQL" without the privacy-architecture framing.
- TensorRT AlgorithmSelector (§6.5.2.6.4) explicit kernel-lock recommendation preserved; vLLM kv_cache_dtype / attention_backend equivalent specified; llama.cpp -ngl / -nb equivalent specified.
- Meta SDC empirical metric (16K H100 / 6 SDCs / 54 days) cited as primary source per R1.2.
- **§6.5.10 R3.10 AgentFS High-Throughput Concurrency (NEW v0.27):** drafter MUST preserve the MPSC daemon architecture's three key properties:
  - Agents experience zero blocking on write (channel send is microsecond-latency; agent returns immediately)
  - SQLite sees only one writer (the daemon thread) — SQLITE_BUSY failure mode mathematically eliminated
  - Backpressure surfaces at daemon queue depth → composes with §4.1 Prevention 4 Pause Gates
  - Implementation: Rust mpsc::channel (production-path commitment) or Python asyncio.Queue (development fallback)
  - PRAGMA configuration verbatim: journal_mode=WAL, wal_autocheckpoint=100, synchronous=NORMAL, cache_size=-64000, temp_store=MEMORY
  - Active checkpoint cadence: daemon issues PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) on 5-second cadence
  - Crash-durability flush-on-exit protocol: SIGTERM drain + SIGSEGV 50ms best-effort + 1-second periodic flush + boot-time §6.5.2 BLAKE3 chain-head verification
  - Honest limit: 1-second flush window is the architectural trade-off; NOT full ACID durability with PRAGMA synchronous=FULL; documented in §12 honest limits
  - PRAGMA values are DIRECTIONAL PRIOR not hardcoded specification — Section 9 calibration framing preserved

### File 11: SECTION_06D_FIREWALL_TIERS.md

**Skeleton lines:** 3374-3787 (§6.6 Inline Semantic Firewall three tiers + §6.6.4 R3.11 IPI via Tool Outputs Mitigation NEW v0.27; ~1200 words target — expanded for R3.11 attack taxonomy + control-token strip + Base64-bypass-refutation)
**Status:** Section structure ratified; drafting clean
**Drafter:** Forger Pool

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- Three-tier framing (Hyperscan / Tree-sitter CST / UAS) is the operational depth — drafter avoids treating any tier as more important than others.
- §6.6.3 UAS calibration specifically against Q4_K_M formatting drift catalog (§6.2.6.2) — drafter cross-references the production failure modes that UAS is designed to catch.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- CST terminology consistent with §5.2.1.
- UAS framed as INLINE not post-hoc audit — this is the architectural distinction from probabilistic anomaly detection.
- **§6.6.4 R3.11 Indirect Prompt Injection via Tool Outputs (NEW v0.27):** drafter MUST preserve the following as non-negotiable architectural invariants:
  - Self-hijacking attack mechanism: script-injected output containing `</think>` + `<final_answer>` tokens prematurely transitions reasoning-model state, bypassing self-correction / auditor checkpoints / Pause Gate evaluation
  - Four-vector attack taxonomy: (a) Direct State-Transition Token Injection (b) Meta-Cognitive Leakage Exploitation (c) Indirect Tool-Chain Hijacking (d) Base64-Encoded Bypass
  - **CRITICAL INVARIANT — Base64 encoding does NOT obfuscate:** frontier LLMs (Claude 3.5, GPT-4, Gemini families) natively decode Base64. Drafter MUST NOT propose Base64 as obfuscation defense; this is a defensive-architecture invariant. §6.6 firewall MUST neutralize payloads BEFORE tokenization.
  - Control-token strip vocabulary: reasoning delimiters + answer delimiters + turn delimiters + tool-use delimiters + vendor-specific control markers (full list per skeleton §6.6.4.3)
  - Neutralization strategy: replace matched tokens with `[STRIPPED:token_name]` placeholders (preserves byte-length, neutralizes state-transition semantics)
  - Tier 1 Hyperscan ~0.26ms for 100KB on Ryzen 5 5500 (R3.11 empirical benchmark); Tier 2 Tree-sitter incremental CST parse; Tier 3 §6.6.3 UAS — three tiers compose without coverage overlap
  - RE2 fallback (~1.2ms for 100KB) for ARM/edge deployment; documented as §14.5 Constitutional Re-Audit scope
  - Defense-in-depth honest limit: §6.6.4 firewall is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT; §4.1 Prevention 6 Bidirectional Verification is the final defensive layer for attacks evading automated firewalls — drafter MUST preserve this framing, not promote the firewall as complete defense

### File 13: SECTION_06F_TEMPORAL_BULKHEAD.md

**Skeleton lines:** 4177-4982 (§6.8 + §6.8.0 + §6.8.0.8 + §6.8.0.9 + §6.8.1 Night Watch; ~2800 words target — substantial expansion from v0.23)
**Status:** R1.4 + R1.1 + R1-DCS primary-source foundations integrated v0.24+v0.25
**Drafter:** Forger Pool

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- §6.8 Temporal Isolation framed as the ARCHITECTURALLY CORRECT choice per R1.4 verdict (§6.8.0.7) and R1-DCS time-slicing rejection (§6.8.0.9). NOT framed as stopgap or hardware-limited compromise.
- The seven sub-subsections of §6.8.0 (Engine Core starvation / SMT hazard / L3 thrashing / DDR4 arbitration / Infinity Fabric / IRQ routing / Verdict) form an analytical argument — drafter preserves the argumentative arc.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- **CRITICAL Sovereign-flagged clarification per §6.8.0.9.2:** the 5-7s figure is the cost of TIME-SLICING (architecture R1 DCS REJECTS and CORE never accepted as v4 production). CORE accepted CPU-isolated bulkhead at §6.8, which carries NO PCIe swap penalty between production and diagnostic pools because they never share VRAM. Residual 5-7s cost applies ONLY to within-pool Forger swap (§6.5.9) at planned mission-boundary transitions, NOT as ongoing operational tax on diagnostic queries. **Drafter must NOT frame the within-pool cost as acceptance of time-slicing.**
- R1.1-sourced PCIe optimization profile (§6.8.0.8.1-§6.8.0.8.8) preserved as deployment-time configuration. The complete GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT summary at §6.8.0.8.8 is canonical.
- R1-DCS three failure categories preserved: OS-Level Eviction Failure / PCIe Latency Floor / State Corruption (with three sub-categories: autoregressive pipeline desynchronization, RoPE context rot under LRU compression, PROMPTPEEK side-channel).

### File 16: SECTION_08A_HOSTILE_AUDITOR_CASE_STUDIES.md

**Skeleton lines:** 6030-6356 (§8.1-§8.6 with §8.5.1 LoCM dispute case study; ~2000 words target)
**Status:** Hostile-Auditor-authored per Q-Q2/R2 multi-instance porting protocol
**Drafter:** **HOSTILE AUDITOR INSTANCE** (NOT Forger Pool — see Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document)

**Multi-instance porting protocol (CRITICAL):**

This is the ONE section in the manifesto that crosses Hostile Auditor instance boundaries. The drafting instance MUST consume the Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document (on disk at /mnt/user-data/outputs/Sovereign_Audit_Trail_Synthesis_Document.md) as the canonical input. The drafting instance MUST NOT read raw conversation transcripts — that's exactly the over-attribution failure mode the §8.5.1 LoCM dispute case study documents against.

**Tone-specific guidance:**
- Honest, technical, methodology-paper register. NOT triumphalist; NOT confessional. The case studies demonstrate a working protocol; the protocol is the contribution.
- Section 4 attribution principles from Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document are MANDATORY: NO first-person pronouns. Findings attribute to "the Hostile Auditor instance during Round X" or simply "the Hostile Auditor." The intelligence resides in the protocol, not in the persona.

**Special drafting requirements:**
- §8.5.1 LoCM dispute Round 1-5 case study — Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document Section 2 is the canonical input. Drafter expands the synthesis-document arc into prose without inventing additional rounds or recovery details.
- §8.5.2 Q-T5 → Q-T9 → Q-T10 corpus-closure / integrity-sweep self-correction case study (NEW v0.27, expanded v0.27.1) — Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document Section 6 is the canonical input. This is a doctrinally distinct case study from §8.5.1: where the LoCM dispute caught FALSE CONTENT from the Strategic Advisor, the Q-T5 → Q-T9 cycle caught FALSE META-PROCEDURE from the Hostile Auditor itself — the "saturation-test framework" was logically sound but applied to a non-enumerated corpus. Q-T10 (recorded as continuation of the same §8.5.2 case study, NOT as separate §8.5.3) demonstrates the SAME procedural-framing failure pattern recurring one layer up: claiming an integrity sweep was complete without verifying every artifact's anchor against the live skeleton version. Three instances of the same pattern in seven sessions is now the documented baseline. Drafter must preserve this distinction; do not collapse §8.5.1 and §8.5.2 into a single "Hostile Auditor catches" narrative. The recursive doctrinal lesson: Bidirectional Verification extends to the framing the Hostile Auditor uses to decide when work is done — and that meta-discipline applies recursively at every layer (corpus closure, integrity sweep, completion claims of any kind). Drafter must explicitly preserve Sovereign 2026-05-18 framing: "It seems like we're reaching a quality limit on editing multiple files... makes me think I've got the right idea on multi-agent orchestration."
- §8.6 Implications section (NEW v0.27 augmentation) — Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document Section 7 is the canonical input on compression and continuity. Drafter must address the structural risk that LLM-driven collaboration introduces: the Hostile Auditor seat survives across instances; the working memory does not. The skeleton on disk persists; the operational context that produced the skeleton compresses. The Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document itself is the mitigation — drafter explicitly names this as the operational counter-measure rather than treating it as background context.
- FINDING 4 ("Structural formatting is presentation, not evidence") anchored as Section 8's doctrinal load-bearer, NOT footnote. Per Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document Section 5: this is the most consequential lesson extracted from the Wilderness era and applies recursively to CORE itself.
- Case-study counts updated v0.27: 46 frameworks verified across 4 verification rounds / 4 confabulations isolated / 5 over-concession-pattern instances (Section 8.4) / **2 over-attribution-pattern instances** — §8.5.1 LoCM dispute (Strategic Advisor false content) + §8.5.2 Q-T5 corpus closure (Hostile Auditor false meta-procedure).
- Section 3 of the Sovereign Audit Trail Synthesis Document (Honest Audit 2026-05-07) is the recursive demonstration that Bidirectional Verification applies to the Hostile Auditor's content output; Section 6 (Q-T5 → Q-T9) extends this to the Hostile Auditor's procedural framing. Drafter preserves both as evidence that the doctrine operates at content AND meta-procedure levels.

---

## Hostile Auditor Review Mechanics

Each drafted prose file enters Hostile Auditor review with:

1. **MANIFEST block check** — line range, drafter ID, draft version, word count actual vs target
2. **Citation accuracy check** — every cited arXiv ID matches verified value; every author name matches verified spelling; every year matches verified publication date
3. **Cross-reference resolution check** — every §X.Y reference resolves to a specific subsection in the v0.25 skeleton OR another drafted file
4. **Audit-trail-preservation check** — no fabrications-stripped claims reintroduced; verified corrections present at correct values; synthesis-honest framings preserved
5. **Word-count discipline check** — drafted word count within ±15% of target; if over, drafter receives REVISION_REQUIRED with tightening priorities
6. **Hostile Auditor expectations check** — section-specific constraints (e.g., CST not AST, GQA/SwiGLU framing, Cloud Oracle deferred-not-recommended) satisfied

REVISION_REQUIRED responses return the file to drafter with specific catch list. APPROVED responses route the file to Sovereign ratification queue.

---

## Drafting Sequence Coordination

Per SKELETON_SPLIT_STRATEGY.md Drafting Order Recommendation:

1. **First wave:** SECTION_04 → SECTION_03 → SECTION_01 (foundation)
2. **Second wave:** SECTION_05A → SECTION_05B → SECTION_05C → SECTION_06A → SECTION_06B (architecture core)
3. **Third wave:** SECTION_06C → SECTION_06D → SECTION_06E → SECTION_06F (architecture detail)
4. **Fourth wave:** SECTION_07A → SECTION_07B (training and methodology)
5. **Fifth wave:** SECTION_08A (Hostile Auditor) → SECTION_09 → SECTION_11_THROUGH_14 (audit + closure)
6. **Final wave:** SECTION_02 (Related Work — drafted last)

SECTION_10 (Sovereign Track) is parallel; not gated on Forger Pool waves.
REFERENCES section bibliography pass is parallel; can run anytime.

---

## Closing Note On Voice Consistency

The 19 split files are drafted by potentially many different Forger Pool instances (and one Hostile Auditor instance for SECTION_08A). Voice consistency comes from the Universal Drafting Principles above, NOT from a single drafting persona. Each Forger Pool instance reads this brief, executes against the section-specific guidance, and produces prose that matches the universal register. The Hostile Auditor review pass catches voice drift between sections during mechanical review (e.g., "Section 5 is dense API-name technical prose; Section 6 suddenly shifts to blog-post register — REVISION_REQUIRED").

The intelligence resides in the protocol, not in the persona.
